summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO provider dispatch API/state handling must not drift between Claude, Codex, and custom provider assumptions in demand evidence mode.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
risks: A provider-specific assumption can silently weaken dispatch or break real agent calls.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: Provider configuration maintains isolation between Claude, Codex, and custom providers in demand evidence mode, with boundary enforcement preventing project mutations and tool restriction flags properly managed per provider.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider function maps provider aliases (anthropic->claude, openai->codex, shell/local->custom) and returns null for auto/empty, preventing provider ambiguity.
evidence.why: Provider normalization is the first line of defense against cross-provider leakage.

evidence.summary: selectedProvider function normalizes provider selection and defaults to claude when provider is empty or normalization fails.
evidence.why: Ensures predictable provider defaults during evidence dispatch.

evidence.summary: Codex provider uses isolated configuration path: codexModel from ai.codex_model or modelValue(ai), codex_sandbox and codex_approval flags, with output-last-message file capture.
evidence.why: Codex has separate command-line flags and model handling from Claude.

evidence.summary: executionConfig clears ai.model when switching to codex if it contains claude, preventing model inheritance across providers.
evidence.why: Explicit cross-provider model contamination prevention.

evidence.summary: Custom provider uses sh -c with rendered command supporting ${model} interpolation, with no explicit file access restrictions in provider-adapter.
evidence.why: Custom providers have different security model than managed providers.

evidence.summary: Claude provider uses separate flags: -p, --model, --permission-mode, --settings, --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools, --disable-slash-commands, --no-session-persistence, --max-budget-usd.
evidence.why: Claude has comprehensive tool and session management flags not used by other providers.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot creates SHA-256 digests for all project files, walking directory tree and using git ls-files when available, with exclusion for .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache.
evidence.why: Foundation for detecting project mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after file digests to detect added, modified, or deleted files, returning sorted change list.
evidence.why: Mutation detection logic that flags boundary violations.

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines forbidden project_mutation and allowed_write_roots restricted to output directory, with agent_instruction boundary_controlled.
evidence.why: Formal boundary policy declaration for evidence agents.

evidence.summary: executionConfig for claude provider in evidence mode disables session persistence (claude_no_session_persistence: true) and clears tool/settings flags (ai.settings='', claude_tools='', claude_allowed_tools='').
evidence.why: Evidence mode imposes strict isolation on Claude sessions.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime captures boundary snapshots before/after agent execution, diffing them to detect violations and adding BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers when changes detected.
evidence.why: Runtime boundary enforcement with actual mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Test verifies codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model (claude regex fails on runOptions.config.ai.model), codex_sandbox and codex_approval remain undefined for flexibility.
evidence.why: Concrete test proving codex configuration isolation from claude defaults.

evidence.summary: Test verifies claude dispatch clears settings, claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools to empty strings, sets claude_no_session_persistence to true, and passes max_budget_usd through.
evidence.why: Test proving Claude tool restriction clearing in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: Tests verify full tool profile requires explicit allowFullAgentTools authorization, and research profile is normalized while keeping tools available.
evidence.why: Tool profile authorization guardrails are tested.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutation tests verify project file writes are blocked even when agent results pass, including git-backed detection of untracked and gitignored files.
evidence.why: Comprehensive boundary enforcement coverage including git edge cases.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary test verifies file walk fallback detects modifications while ignoring artifacts in allowed write roots.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement works without git repository.

evidence.summary: buildAgentAdapterCapabilities returns provider-specific capability contracts including sandbox modes, file_write/shell_exec permissions, and budget enforceability per provider.
evidence.why: Provider capabilities are formally separated and documented.

assumptions: Custom provider commands are assumed to be trusted by the user when explicitly configured in ai.custom_command.
Provider-specific defaults (like codex_sandbox workspace-write) are assumed safe for evidence mode workloads.
Git ls-files availability is assumed for optimal boundary detection, with file walk fallback when git is unavailable.
risks: A provider-specific bug in model field clearing (line 448) could cause cross-provider model leakage.
Custom provider with arbitrary sh -c commands could bypass intended boundary restrictions if configured maliciously.
Boundary detection might miss file mutations in very large repositories with slow git operations or excluded directories.

result.notes: Provider configuration maintains isolation through separate code paths, explicit model clearing, and boundary enforcement. Test coverage validates cross-provider separation and mutation detection. Risks are mitigated through design and testing.

agent_results.claim: Provider configuration maintains isolation between Claude, Codex, and custom providers through normalized provider selection, separate configuration paths, explicit model clearing, and boundary enforcement. Test coverage validates cross-provider separation, boundary mutation detection, and tool restriction clearing in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: PROVIDER_ALIASES maps anthropic/openai to claude/codex, and normalizeAgentProvider converts provider inputs to normalized values, returning null for auto/empty to prevent provider ambiguity.
evidence.why: Provider normalization is the first defense against cross-provider leakage.

evidence.summary: selectedProvider normalizes provider value and defaults to claude when empty or normalization fails, ensuring predictable provider selection.
evidence.why: Default behavior prevents undefined provider states during dispatch.

evidence.summary: Codex provider uses isolated configuration: codexModel from ai.codex_model or modelValue(ai), codex_sandbox defaults to workspace-write, codex_approval defaults to never, with output-last-message file capture.
evidence.why: Codex has completely separate configuration path from Claude.

evidence.summary: Model field clearing logic checks if provider is codex and ai.model contains claude, then clears ai.model to empty string to prevent cross-provider model contamination.
evidence.why: Explicit cross-provider model contamination prevention at configuration level.

evidence.summary: Custom provider uses sh -c with rendered command supporting ${model} interpolation via renderCustomCommand, with no explicit file access restrictions in provider-adapter.
evidence.why: Custom providers have different security model than managed providers.

evidence.summary: Claude provider uses comprehensive flags: -p, --model, --permission-mode, --settings, --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools, --disable-slash-commands, --no-session-persistence, --max-budget-usd.
evidence.why: Claude has extensive tool and session management not used by other providers.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot creates SHA-256 digests for all project files using git ls-files when available or file walk fallback, excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache directories.
evidence.why: Foundation for detecting project mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after file digests to detect added, modified, or deleted files, returning sorted change list with path and change type.
evidence.why: Mutation detection logic that flags boundary violations.

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines project_mutation as forbidden, restricts allowed_write_roots to output directory, sets agent_instruction to boundary_controlled.
evidence.why: Formal boundary policy declaration for evidence agents.

evidence.summary: executionConfig for claude provider in evidence mode disables session persistence (claude_no_session_persistence: true), clears tool/settings flags (ai.settings, claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools to empty/defaults).
evidence.why: Evidence mode imposes strict isolation on Claude sessions.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime captures boundary snapshots before/after agent execution, diffs them to detect violations, adds BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers when changes detected.
evidence.why: Runtime boundary enforcement with actual mutation detection and blocker injection.

evidence.summary: Test verifies codex dispatch: provider is codex, codex_sandbox and codex_approval remain undefined, ai.model does not contain claude (regex assertion fails).
evidence.why: Concrete test proving codex configuration isolation from claude defaults and model clearing.

evidence.summary: Test verifies claude dispatch: settings, claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools are empty strings, claude_no_session_persistence is true, max_budget_usd passes through.
evidence.why: Test proving Claude tool restriction clearing in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: Tests verify full tool profile requires allowFullAgentTools authorization, research profile is normalized while keeping tools available, preventing unauthorized tool access.
evidence.why: Tool profile authorization guardrails prevent capability escalation.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutation tests verify project file writes are blocked even when agent results pass, including git-backed detection of untracked and gitignored files.
evidence.why: Comprehensive boundary enforcement coverage including git edge cases.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary test verifies file walk fallback detects modifications while ignoring artifacts in allowed write roots (.yolo/demand/evidence/).
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement works without git repository.

evidence.summary: buildAgentAdapterCapabilities returns provider-specific capability contracts including sandbox modes, file_write/shell_exec permissions, and budget enforceability per provider.
evidence.why: Provider capabilities are formally separated and documented.

assumptions: Custom provider commands are assumed to be trusted by the user when explicitly configured in ai.custom_command.
Provider-specific defaults (codex_sandbox workspace-write, claude_permission_mode default) are assumed safe for evidence mode workloads.
Git ls-files availability is assumed for optimal boundary detection, with file walk fallback when git is unavailable.
risks: Provider-specific model clearing logic at provider-adapter.ts:447-449 has test coverage confirming it works, but any future refactoring could introduce cross-provider model leakage.
Custom provider with arbitrary sh -c commands could bypass intended boundary restrictions if configured maliciously, though this is a user-configured trust boundary.
Boundary detection might miss file mutations in very large repositories with slow git operations or excluded directories, though file walk fallback provides reasonable coverage.

result.notes: Independent verification confirms all explorer findings. Provider isolation is maintained through separate code paths, normalization, explicit model clearing, and boundary enforcement. Test coverage validates cross-provider separation, mutation detection, and tool restrictions. No conflicts found with explorer evidence.

agent_results.claim: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness for provider isolation and boundary enforcement in demand evidence mode. All factual claims are backed by project-scoped evidence with usable paths, assumptions are properly declared, and no unresolved missing data remains.

evidence.summary: Model clearing logic at provider-adapter.ts:447-449 explicitly prevents cross-provider contamination by clearing ai.model when switching to codex if it contains claude.
evidence.why: Verifies that the cross-checker challenge about model clearing being untested is incorrect; test exists at demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts:198-232.

evidence.summary: Test suite provides comprehensive coverage for codex isolation, claude tool restrictions, boundary mutation detection with git, and boundary fallback without git.
evidence.why: Confirms that all critical isolation and boundary enforcement paths have test coverage.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime implements actual boundary snapshot diffing with blocker injection, not just policy declaration.
evidence.why: Verifies that boundary enforcement is operational, not just theoretical.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider and buildAgentAdapterCapabilities provide formal provider separation and capability documentation.
evidence.why: Confirms provider normalization is implemented at contract level.

risks: If any evidence paths are incorrect or files have moved since exploration, verification would fail. However, all paths follow the established project structure and are cross-verified between explorer and cross-checker.
If the demand requires runtime stress testing with actual Claude agent invocations (as suggested in status_quo), that evidence is not present in current results.

result.notes: Evidence from explorer and cross-checker is consistent, well-sourced, and sufficient for PRD readiness on provider configuration and boundary enforcement. All assumptions are properly declared, no project claims are made without evidence, and test coverage validates critical paths. Evidence agreement status is pass with no conflicts. The only potential gap is runtime stress testing with real Claude agents if that is required by the acceptance criteria.
